tasks.title: Provision v3.6 activation and evidence environments
tasks.description: Provision the external runner, package, authentication, and publication environments required to turn the v3.6 repository implementation into same-revision fixed-runner and immutable release evidence. Linux x64, Linux ARM64 and macOS ARM64 runners, persistent strict-offline Conan graphs, native evidence workflows, tag publication and independent runtime-asset verification are complete. The accepted macOS ADR deliberately ships the first command-line/server archive with signed=false and notarized=false, so Apple Developer ID credentials are not a v3.6.0 activation requirement.
acceptance_criteria: The macOS signed=false and notarized=false distribution boundary is approved by the platform ADR and disclosed in the public v3.6.0 release notes.
Release publication credentials and repository protections permit the immutable v3.6.0 tag workflow and post-release verification.
The final publication and three-platform independent runtime-asset verification run identifiers are recorded before this TODO is completed.

tasks.title: Close Linux ARM64 and macOS ARM64 production evidence
tasks.description: Linux ARM64 and macOS ARM64 completed native production evidence on frozen revision 79930cc2fe21aafa71d34cc6631315373d8b27ae. Both platforms have exact-SHA Release/R0/R5/JWKS/SDK/symbol candidate evidence, repeated baseline, capacity/R4, native 2h soak and readiness. Runtime archives and SPDX SBOMs were published by v3.6.0 run 30025684329 and independently verified on Linux ARM64 run 30026727913 and macOS ARM64 run 30027061993. SDK registry/GitHub assets and independent symbol assets remain separately blocked activation tracks and are not claimed as v3.6.0 published assets.
acceptance_criteria: Platform-specific soak, capacity, and performance baselines pass for Linux ARM64 and macOS ARM64 on one frozen candidate SHA.
Release, R0, R5, JWKS, debug-symbol and SDK distribution workflows refresh separate linux-arm64 and macos-arm64 artifacts on that SHA.
Both ARM platforms pass readiness and independent published runtime-asset verification; unsigned/unnotarized macOS and separately blocked SDK/symbol capabilities are disclosed explicitly.

tasks.title: Review and integrate Linux runner changes
tasks.description: After the Linux runner changes are committed and pushed, fetch the exact remote branch or commit, review and integrate it locally, validate the combined platform evidence implementation, and use the resulting facts to reprioritize the remaining production-platform work.
acceptance_criteria: The Linux runner branch name and exact commit SHA are recorded before integration starts.

The remote changes are fetched without overwriting unrelated local work and are reviewed for runner labels, persistent Conan cache identity, Linux architecture handling, Docker image platform, R5 evidence, workflow security, and documentation consistency.
Merge or rebase conflicts are resolved without weakening linux-x64, linux-arm64, or macos-arm64 platform boundaries.
Relevant Python tests, Conan governance, workflow catalog, fixed-runner plan, Docker R5 policy checks, and any runner-specific validation introduced by the change all pass on the integrated revision.
TODO-0002 and the project priority plan are updated from the integrated runner evidence, with completed items checked only when supported by exact-SHA artifacts.

tasks.title: Execute parallel Linux and macOS production evidence tracks
tasks.description: Execute platform-specific evidence without substituting results across linux-x64, linux-arm64 and macos-arm64. Frozen revision 79930cc2fe21aafa71d34cc6631315373d8b27ae completed all three readiness decisions, tag Release run 30025684329 published three native runtime archives and SPDX SBOMs, and independent verification passed on each platform. SDK 4.2.0 packages and debug-symbol/dSYM bundles retain exact-SHA candidate evidence but remain outside the v3.6.0 release manifest and default supported asset matrix.
acceptance_criteria: After repository changes stop, one candidate SHA is frozen and every claimed platform refreshes its required summaries and immutable assets on that exact SHA.
Production readiness rejects cross-platform or cross-SHA evidence and records remaining unsupported platform capabilities explicitly.

The v3.6.0 runtime tag assets pass independent checksum, SBOM, provenance and package-consumer verification on every claimed platform; SDK and symbol assets are not claimed published and remain tracked separately.

tasks.title: Increase Linux ARM64 performance headroom
tasks.description: Linux ARM64 attribution run 30058635595 isolated the historical tail to Gateway route queue/topology, not backend CPU. Isolated runs 30059535441 and 30061707672 used pool/workers 8/8 and disjoint service/loadgen CPU sets; battle-100 P99 was 10/10/10ms with zero rejected/failed and the 250ms gate unchanged. The optimization shipped only in immutable v3.6.2.
acceptance_criteria: A fixed isolated Linux ARM64 run records service/load-generator resources and at least three valid battle-100 repetitions with no infrastructure contention.
The cause of the 300ms tail sample is attributed to runtime, kernel/VM scheduling, topology, or measurement granularity with reproducible evidence.
Any optimization preserves the 250ms gate, passes the full platform regression matrix, and is released only in a new version.

tasks.title: Publish immutable SDK and symbol assets
tasks.description: Immutable v3.6.2 Release run 30063021104 published 25 runtime, symbol/dSYM, SDK 4.2.0 wheel, multi-RID NuGet, SPDX, provenance and checksum assets. Linux x64, Linux ARM64 and macOS ARM64 independent published-asset runs 30063950242, 30063441646 and 30063444082 passed; PyPI/NuGet.org and Apple notarization remain separate activation boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: The release workflow publishes supported wheel/NuGet and runtime/symbol pairs with platform-specific names, checksums, SPDX SBOMs, provenance and SBOM attestations.
Published-asset verification independently downloads each package, validates RID or build-id/UUID binding, and runs a clean no-source consumer or symbolization probe.
Compatibility and release notes distinguish supported assets from candidate RIDs, PyPI/NuGet.org remain blocked without trusted publishing, and v3.6.0 assets are never overwritten.
